metadata.title: PMDA Guideline on Drug Interaction for Drug Development and Appropriate Provision of Information
metadata.note: Japan-specific drug interaction guideline. Filename is misleading - actual content is drug interaction guidance, not bioequivalence for generics. Contains Japan-specific requirements for in vitro studies, clinical DDI studies, transporter evaluations, and package insert labeling rules distinct from ICH M12 and EMA DDI guidelines.

rules.rule_text: In vitro studies using human tissue-derived specimen and expression systems of drug metabolizing enzymes and transporters must be conducted to investigate basic factors contributing to drug interactions and the possibility of drug interactions occurring in clinical practice.
rules.source_section: 1.3 Principles of drug interaction studies
rules.verbatim_quote: in vitro studies using human tissue-derived specimen and expression systems of drug metabolizing enzymes and transporters should be conducted to investigate the basic factors contributing to drug interactions and the possibility of drug interactions occurring in the clinical practice.

rules.rule_text: Drug interaction studies must be carried out in a stepwise manner based on the phase of development. In vitro studies evaluating effects on the investigational drug and its effects on concomitant drugs must be completed before Phase III studies begin.
rules.source_section: 1.3 Principles of drug interaction studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Drug interaction studies in the drug development process should be carried out in a stepwise manner based on the phase of development. In vitro studies evaluating the possibility for the effects of concomitant drugs on the investigational drug and the effects of the investigational drug on concomitant drugs should be carried out before the implementation of studies in a large number of patients or long-term administration (usually before the initiation of phase III studies).

rules.rule_text: Prior to Phase I studies, plasma protein binding and drug metabolism information must be obtained based on in vitro studies. Results of clinical DDI studies and mass balance studies should be obtained before initiation of Phase III.
rules.source_section: 1.3 Principles of drug interaction studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Usually, prior to the initiation of phase I studies, the information of plasma (serum) protein binding and drug metabolism of the investigational drug should be obtained based on in vitro studies... results of clinical drug interaction studies and mass balance studies should be obtained before the initiation of phase III studies.

rules.rule_text: Clinical drug interaction studies must be conducted in compliance with GCP, and pharmacokinetic drug interactions must be evaluated in accordance with the PMDA Clinical Pharmacokinetic Studies on Pharmaceuticals notification.
rules.source_section: 1.3 Principles of drug interaction studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical drug interaction studies should be conducted in compliance with Good Clinical Practice (Standards for the Conduct of Clinical Trials of Medical Products) (GCP), and pharmacokinetic drug interactions should be evaluated in accordance with "Clinical Pharmacokinetic Studies on Pharmaceuticals."

rules.rule_text: Food effects on drug absorption must be examined using the final formulation, as influences vary among different formulations.
rules.source_section: 2. Drug interactions in absorption
rules.verbatim_quote: The influences of food on the drug absorption process should be examined using the final formulation of the drug, because the influences vary among different formulations.

rules.rule_text: For drugs with pH-dependent solubility, the need for clinical DDI studies with drugs that change gastric pH (PPIs, H2 receptor antagonists, antacids) on GI absorption must be considered.
rules.source_section: 2.1.1 Effects of concomitant drugs on the investigational drug
rules.verbatim_quote: If the investigational drug has pH-dependent solubility, the need for implementation of clinical drug interaction studies with concomitant drugs that cause changes in the gastric pH (proton pump inhibitors, H2 receptor antagonists, antacids, etc.) on the GI absorption should be considered.

rules.rule_text: For pediatric drugs, characteristics of foods consumed by neonates and infants (such as milk) must be taken into consideration when evaluating food-drug interactions.
rules.source_section: 2.1.1 Effects of concomitant drugs on the investigational drug
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of pediatric drugs, the characteristics of the foods, such as ingestion of milk in neonates and infants should also be taken into consideration.

rules.rule_text: The possibility of working as a substrate of P-gp and BCRP must be evaluated for all investigational drugs by in vitro studies in principle, as these transporters affect oral bioavailability.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Studies to examine drug interactions mediated by transporters involved in absorption
rules.verbatim_quote: the possibility of working as a substrate of P-gp and BCRP should be evaluated for all investigational drugs by in vitro studies in principle.
rules.exceptions: Drugs whose routes of administration are other than oral route need to be examined in some cases due to effects on elimination and brain distribution.

rules.rule_text: In vitro P-gp and BCRP studies must be performed using experimental systems with transporter activity verified by typical substrates and typical inhibitors. Bidirectional transcellular transport studies using Caco-2 cells or overexpressing cell lines are desirable.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Studies to examine drug interactions mediated by transporters involved in absorption
rules.verbatim_quote: in vitro studies should be performed using the experimental system with the transporter activity checked by using typical substrates and typical inhibitors... it is desirable to conduct bidirectional transcellular transport studies using Caco-2 cells or other cell lines over-expressing particular transporters.

rules.rule_text: Drugs mainly eliminated via hepatic metabolism or biliary excretion (either route accounting for 25% or more of total clearance) must be assessed for whether they are substrates of hepatic uptake transporters OATP1B1 and OATP1B3.
rules.source_section: 6.3 Studies to examine drug interactions mediated by transporters in the liver
rules.verbatim_quote: Investigational drugs which are mainly eliminated via hepatic metabolism or biliary excretion (i.e., clearance via either route accounting for 25% or more of the total clearance) should be assessed to examine whether they are substrates for the hepatic uptake transporters OATP1B1 and 1B3.

rules.rule_text: Drugs whose major route of elimination is active renal secretion (accounting for 25% or more of total clearance) must be assessed for whether they are substrates of OAT1, OAT3, OCT2, MATE1, or MATE2-K.
rules.source_section: 6.4 Studies to examine drug interactions mediated by transporters in the kidney
rules.verbatim_quote: Investigational drugs whose major route of elimination is active renal secretion (renal secretion clearance accounting for 25% or more of the total clearance), should be assessed to examine whether they are substrates of OAT1, OAT3, OCT2, MATE1 or MATE2-K.

rules.rule_text: Clinical drug interaction studies should be conducted mainly in healthy volunteers, prior to Phase III studies, in principle. Clinical doses of the investigational drug, clinical index drug, inhibitor, and inducer must be used.
rules.source_section: 7.1 Need for and timing of clinical drug interaction studies
rules.verbatim_quote: it is recommended that a clinical drug interaction study(s) is conducted mainly in healthy volunteers, prior to phase III studies, in principle. Drug interaction studies should be conducted using clinical doses of the investigational drug, clinical index drug, inhibitor, and inducer.
rules.exceptions: When it is difficult to perform studies in healthy volunteers, drug interaction studies may be performed in indicated patients.

rules.rule_text: If in vitro DDI studies suggest interaction possibility with a concomitant drug, use of that concomitant drug must be contraindicated in principle until safety is demonstrated by a clinical DDI study.
rules.source_section: 7.1 Need for and timing of clinical drug interaction studies
rules.verbatim_quote: If in vitro drug interaction studies suggest the possibility of interaction with a concomitant drug, use of the concomitant drug in question should be contraindicated in principle, until its safety is demonstrated by a clinical drug interaction study, etc.

rules.rule_text: The presence or absence of drug interaction must be judged based on the 90% confidence interval of the geometric mean ratio of PK parameters. When the 90% CI falls within 0.80-1.25, no pharmacokinetic interaction is generally concluded.
rules.source_section: 7.2 Relevant indices of drug interactions and pharmacokinetic parameters
rules.verbatim_quote: The presence/absence of drug interaction should be judged based on the results of clinical drug interaction studies, in terms of the 90% confidence interval of the geometric mean ratio of pharmacokinetic parameters obtained with and without co-administration of the interacting drug. When the 90% confidence interval of the geometric mean ratio falls in the range of 0.80-1.25, it is generally judged that there are no pharmacokinetic interactions between the drugs in question.
rules.exceptions: Whether or not the above value falls in the range, it should also be considered whether the drug interaction is a clinical concern based on the safety profile.

rules.rule_text: Clinical DDI studies must use randomized cross-over design, add-on design, or equivalent. Parallel-group comparison design is not recommended. Comparison with historical controls is unjustifiable in principle.
rules.source_section: 7.3 Study design
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical drug interaction studies are carried out using randomized cross-over design, add-on design... The parallel-group comparison design is not recommended usually because it is necessary to consider the influence of inter-individual difference. Comparison with a control group in the separate study (historical controls) is unjustifiable in principle.

rules.rule_text: Subjects who have used ethical drugs, OTC drugs, supplements, health food products, tobacco, or alcohol prior to registration must be considered for exclusion from clinical DDI studies.
rules.source_section: 7.3 Study design
rules.verbatim_quote: Subjects who have ingested ethical drugs, over-the-counter drugs, supplements, health food products, tobacco, or alcohol prior to the registration should be considered to be excluded from the clinical drug interaction studies because these substances may alter the activities of drug metabolizing enzymes and transporters.

rules.rule_text: For DDI studies, inhibitors and inducers must be dosed at their expected or approved maximum doses with minimum dosing intervals to maximize the possibility of detecting drug interactions.
rules.source_section: 7.4 Dose and route of administration
rules.verbatim_quote: The dose and regimen of inhibitors or inducers used in the study should be those that maximize the possibility of drug interactions, and the expected or approved maximum doses and minimum intervals of administration should be used.
rules.exceptions: For substrates, any dose in the linear range can be used. If the substrate has non-linear pharmacokinetics, the dose should be determined with consideration of the therapeutic dose.

rules.rule_text: The route of administration chosen for clinical DDI studies for the investigational drug must generally be the one expected in clinical practice.
rules.source_section: 7.4 Dose and route of administration
rules.verbatim_quote: For an investigational drug, the route of administration generally should be the one expected in clinical practice.
rules.exceptions: If only oral dosage forms will be marketed, studies with an intravenous formulation are not usually necessary.

rules.rule_text: A strong inhibitor (AUC increase >=5-fold or CL/F decrease to <1/5) is defined based on the degree of influence on AUC of sensitive substrate in clinical DDI studies. Strong inhibitors of drug metabolizing enzymes involved in the elimination of the investigational drug must be used first in clinical DDI studies.
rules.source_section: 7.6.1 Clinical drug interaction studies using inhibitors of cytochrome P450
rules.verbatim_quote: When an inhibitor is considered to increase the AUC by >=5-fold (or a decrease in the CL/F to less than 1/5), the drug is termed as a "strong inhibitor"... In the implementation of clinical drug interaction studies, a strong inhibitor... should be used first as much as possible to evaluate the degree of changes in the pharmacokinetics of the investigational drug.
rules.exceptions: In cases that it is difficult to conduct clinical drug interaction studies using strong inhibitors from the point of view of safety concerns, the effects of moderate or weak inhibitors may be evaluated.

rules.rule_text: A moderate inhibitor is defined as one that increases AUC >=2-fold but <5-fold (CL/F decrease to <1/2 but >=1/5). A weak inhibitor increases AUC >=1.25-fold but <2-fold (CL/F decrease to <1/1.25 but >=1/2).
rules.source_section: 7.6.1 Clinical drug interaction studies using inhibitors of cytochrome P450
rules.verbatim_quote: an inhibitor that is considered to cause an increase in the AUC by >=2-fold but <5-fold (or a decrease in the CL/F to <1/2 but >=1/5) is termed as a "moderate inhibitor", and an inhibitor that is considered to cause an increase in the AUC by >=1.25-fold but <2-fold (or a decrease in the CL/F to <1/1.25 but >=1/2) is termed as a "weak inhibitor".

rules.rule_text: A strong inducer is defined as one that reduces AUC to 1/5 or less (or increases CL/F ratio >5-fold). A moderate inducer reduces AUC to <=1/2 but >1/5 (CL/F increase >=2-fold but <5-fold). A weak inducer reduces AUC to <1/1.25 but >1/2 (CL/F increase >=1.25-fold but <2-fold).
rules.source_section: 7.7 Selection of inducers for drug metabolizing enzymes
rules.verbatim_quote: When an inducer is considered to reduce the AUC to 1/5 or less (or increase the CL/F ratio by >5-fold), the drug is termed as a "strong inducer". Likewise, an inducer that is considered to reduce the AUC to <=1/2 but >1/5 (or increase the CL/F by >=2-fold but <5-fold) is termed as a "moderate inducer", and an inducer that is considered to reduce the AUC by <1/1.25 but >1/2 (or increase the CL/F by >=1.25-fold but <2-fold) is termed as a "weak inducer".

rules.rule_text: For assessing whether the investigational drug inhibits or induces a drug metabolizing enzyme (or transporter), a clinical DDI study must use an index drug or typical substrate with high selectivity and whose contribution to total elimination is high (sensitive substrate).
rules.source_section: 7.8 Selection of substrates for drug metabolizing enzymes and transporters
rules.verbatim_quote: For assessing by clinical studies whether an investigational drug inhibits or induces a drug metabolizing enzyme (or a transporter) or not, a clinical drug interaction study should be performed using an index drug or a typical substrate for the transporter which has a high selectivity for specific drug metabolizing enzyme (or transporter) and its contribution to total elimination is high (sensitive substrate).

rules.rule_text: Cocktail substrate studies used for DDI evaluation must use index drugs or sensitive substrates susceptible to interactions for each target enzyme (and transporter). The effect on AUC must be calculated for each substrate.
rules.source_section: 7.9.3 Cocktail substrate studies
rules.verbatim_quote: The substrates used in cocktail substrate studies should consist of index drug or sensitive substrates susceptible to interactions for each target enzyme (and transporter). For each index drug or substrate used, the effect of the investigational drug on AUC should be calculated.

rules.rule_text: For drugs whose major elimination pathway is mediated by CYP2C9, CYP2C19, CYP2D6, UGT1A1, OATP1B1, or BCRP, genetic polymorphism analysis should be performed prior to clinical DDI studies, as these enzymes/transporters show pharmacokinetic variability by genotype.
rules.source_section: 7.9.5.1 Evaluation of drug interactions in consideration of genetic polymorphism
rules.verbatim_quote: Molecular species of drug metabolizing enzymes and transporters with pharmacokinetics that are greatly affected by the genetic polymorphism include CYP2C9, CYP2C19, CYP2D6, UGT1A1, OATP1B1 and BCRP. For drugs whose major elimination pathway is mediated by these drug metabolizing enzymes or transporters, it is useful to perform genetic polymorphism analysis prior to clinical drug interaction studies.

rules.rule_text: CYP2C19 and CYP2D6 genetic polymorphisms causing defective activity of the former and marked reduction of the latter are frequent among East Asians. This must be considered when designing DDI studies for drugs eliminated via these pathways.
rules.source_section: 7.9.5.1 Evaluation of drug interactions in consideration of genetic polymorphism
rules.verbatim_quote: genetic polymorphisms that cause the defective activity of the former enzyme and marked reduction of the activity of the latter enzyme are frequent among East Asians.

rules.rule_text: When the CR of a specific enzyme/transporter pathway to the overall elimination of the investigational drug is estimated at 25% or more, clinical DDI studies using drugs that affect that enzyme must be considered.
rules.source_section: 4.1 Evaluation of the possibility of the investigational drug as an affected drug
rules.verbatim_quote: the CR of the elimination pathway controlled by a certain drug metabolizing enzyme to the overall elimination of the investigational drug is estimated to be 25% or more, implementation of clinical drug interaction studies using drugs that affect the enzyme in question... should be considered.

rules.rule_text: In vitro metabolism studies must determine whether the investigational drug exerts an inhibitory effect on P450. The R value (ratio of intrinsic clearance with/without the investigational drug) must be calculated and compared with cutoff values to determine clinical DDI study need.
rules.source_section: 4.2 Evaluation of the possibility of the investigational drug inhibiting drug metabolizing enzymes
rules.verbatim_quote: In vitro metabolism studies should be carried out to determine whether the investigational drug exerts an inhibitory effect on P450. To determine whether or not clinical drug interaction studies should be conducted to examine the possibility of the investigational drug acting as an inhibitor, the ratio of intrinsic clearance value of the substrate (R value) under the presence and absence of the investigational drug for specific enzyme reaction (R value) should be calculated and compared with the cutoff values.

rules.rule_text: The enzyme inhibition effects of major metabolites must be investigated in addition to the unchanged drug. Target metabolites should be chosen based on systemic exposure and chemical structure similarity to the parent drug.
rules.source_section: 4.2 Evaluation of the possibility of the investigational drug inhibiting drug metabolizing enzymes
rules.verbatim_quote: It is desirable to investigate the enzyme inhibition effects of major metabolites in addition to those of the unchanged drug. Target metabolites should be chosen based on the evaluation from the viewpoints of systemic exposure and chemical structure compared with the unchanged drug.

rules.rule_text: The possibility of the investigational drug inducing drug metabolizing enzymes must be investigated. Whether to implement clinical DDI studies for enzyme induction potential should be determined by cutoff values based on in vitro data. MSPK model and PBPK model may also be used.
rules.source_section: 4.3 Evaluation of the possibility of the investigational drug inducing drug metabolizing enzymes
rules.verbatim_quote: the possibility of drug interactions should be investigated. In general, the need for clinical drug interaction studies is examined based on the results of in vitro metabolism studies... Whether to implement clinical drug interaction studies to evaluate the possibility of the investigational drug acting as an inducer should be determined by the cutoff values based on in vitro data, etc.

rules.rule_text: For drugs metabolized by enzymes other than P450 (MAO, FMO, XO, AO, alcohol/aldehyde dehydrogenase), if their contribution to clearance is large, the enzymes/isoenzymes must be identified and the degree of contribution clarified.
rules.source_section: 4.4 Drug interactions mediated by enzymes other than cytochrome P450
rules.verbatim_quote: When the investigational drug is the substrate of these phase I enzymes other than P450 and their contribution to the clearance is large, identification of the enzymes/isoenzymes involved and determination of the degree of contribution are also recommended.

rules.rule_text: For UGT-metabolized drugs, the inhibitory effect of the investigational drug must be examined not only on the isoenzymes mainly involved in its own metabolism but also on UGT1A1 and UGT2B7 which are involved in metabolism of a large number of medical products.
rules.source_section: 4.4 Drug interactions mediated by enzymes other than cytochrome P450
rules.verbatim_quote: examination of the inhibitory effect of the investigational drug is recommended not only on the isoenzymes of UGT mainly involved in its metabolism but also on isoenzymes known to be involved in metabolism of a relatively large number of medical products (e.g., UGT1A1 and UGT2B7)

rules.rule_text: If the investigational drug is a cytokine or cytokine modifier, the need for clinical DDI studies to evaluate effects on P450 or transporters must be considered from the viewpoint of efficacy and safety.
rules.source_section: 4.5 Drug interactions with biotechnological/biological products
rules.verbatim_quote: If the investigational drug is a cytokine or a cytokine modifier, the need for implementation of clinical drug interaction studies to evaluate the effects of the investigational drug on P450 or transporter should be considered, from the viewpoint of the efficacy and safety of the investigational drug and concomitant drugs.

rules.rule_text: Package insert alert categories for DDI must be classified as either 'Contraindications For Co-Administration (Do not co-administer)' when serious adverse reactions are anticipated from significant PK changes, or 'Precautions For Co-Administration (Be careful about concomitant use)' when risk is manageable.
rules.source_section: 8.1 Description in Precautions in drug package inserts
rules.verbatim_quote: The alerting actions are classified into either "Contraindications For Co-Administration (Do not co-administer)" or "Precautions For Co-Administration (Be careful about concomitant use)." When the serious adverse drug reactions resulting from significant pharmacokinetic changes are anticipated realistically, and the seriousness of the consequence would outweigh expected therapeutic effects, it should be considered to make the combination contraindicated.

rules.rule_text: In the INTERACTIONS section of the package insert, for CYP3A-mediated DDIs, the strength classification of inhibition/induction and non-proprietary names of approximately three representative concomitant drugs requiring special attention must be listed.
rules.source_section: 8.2 Description in Drugs in the section of INTERACTIONS
rules.verbatim_quote: the strength classification of inhibition or induction should be described at the beginning in the section "INTERACTIONS" and each of non-proprietary names of about three representative concomitant drugs (drug metabolized by CYP3A) requiring special attention should be listed in "Drugs" in "Precautions For Co-Administration", in consideration of possibility of concomitant use in clinical practice.
rules.exceptions: For P450 isoenzymes other than CYP3A, the strength classification of inhibition or induction should be described only when necessary.

rules.rule_text: If dosage adjustment is necessary to compensate for drug interactions, the adjustment must be described specifically in the PRECAUTIONS CONCERNING DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION section based on quantitative information from clinical interaction studies.
rules.source_section: 8.1 Description in Precautions in drug package inserts
rules.verbatim_quote: If adjustment of the dosage regimen of the investigational drug is necessary to compensate the drug interactions, the adjustment should be described specifically in the "PRECAUTIONS CONCERNING DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION" based on the quantitative information collected from conducted clinical interaction studies, etc.
